artPrompt: A hunched goblin rogue clutching a plain brown envelope to his chest in a rain-slick alley, eyes darting, amber lamplight pooling on wet cobbles below him in the wide frame. Cigar smoke curls into ink-shadow above, a shadowed handshake half-suggested at the margin. All furtive gesture, the envelope the only certainty. Intimate criminal menace, rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Back-Room Bagman
text: When Back-Room Bagman enters the battlefield, target opponent gains 2 life and you create a Favor token. (A Favor is a colorless artifact token with "Sacrifice this artifact: Choose a player who owes you a Favor. That player loses 1 life or sacrifices a permanent.")
flavorText: He carries the brown envelope. He does not know what is in the brown envelope. He prefers it that way.
